scope: nexus external-API bridge subsystem (16 hexa-sim bridges + bridge_health.sh checker + bridge_sha256.tsv R1 baseline)
approach: raw#9 hexa-only read-only audit ($0); no bridge invocation (avoids hitting external APIs); cross-reference bridge_health.sh + design/hexa_sim/2026-04-26_bridge_health_check.md + state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl + bridge_sha256.tsv

trigger.event: hexa-lang commit fe20e3dc adds curl + wget to hexa-runner Dockerfile
trigger.motivation: 7/16 bridge_health FAIL (2026-04-26 00:00 UTC) all root-caused to 'sh: 1: curl: not found' inside docker container
trigger.expected_effect: container-side curl makes all 6 requires-live FAILs PASS; uniprot still FAIL (sentinel-bug, not curl)

inventory_16_bridges.comment_on_count: Survey shows 16 production bridges (not 7+9=16, not 7/16 + 9 separate). The '9 more' phrasing in the task prompt likely conflated 7 FAILs vs 9 PASSes; total=16.

status_classifier.PASS: ec=0 + sentinel matches (__NAME_BRIDGE__ PASS or [name_bridge selftest] OK)
status_classifier.FAIL: ec=0 with no sentinel | ec!=0 with no fallback marker
status_classifier.TAMPERED: R1 sha256_16 of bridge file != pinned baseline in state/bridge_sha256.tsv

health_check_design.exit_code: 76 if any FAIL/TAMPERED (raw 23 schema-guard analog); 0 if all PASS
health_check_design.R1_per_bridge_sha: state/bridge_sha256.tsv pins 16 bridges to first-16-hex sha256; mismatch fires TAMPERED. Defends against silent .hexa rewrite (sentinel-injection into mutated body). Propagated from falsifier_health.sh.

pre_curl_baseline.root_cause_uniform: hexa-runner:latest container has no `curl` binary — exec("curl ...") returns empty body
pre_curl_baseline.edge_case_uniprot: fallback data populates fields but sentinel emits ok=false because fetch was attempted-and-empty (sentinel-classification bug, not curl)

post_curl_projection.hidden_fallback_unmasking: codata/horizons/cmb/etc. were silently relying on hardcoded fallback because curl returned empty; with curl present they will start hitting live endpoints — could expose latent rate-limit / endpoint-drift issues that fallbacks were masking

stable_history.2026-04-25T18:14_to_23:40: 16/16 PASS (3 offline_fallback) on Mac shell — bridge_health.sh ran outside docker container with system curl
stable_history.transient_TAMPERED_episodes: openalex (23:00) + lhc (23:21-23:38) sha mismatches resolved via baseline refresh (commits 4a8eb529, 7379ec17 R5 chain)

recent_changes_to_bridge_subsystem.2026-04-26_R1_propagation: F1: bridge_sha256 verification wired (TAMPERED status); commit 1836dd20
recent_changes_to_bridge_subsystem.2026-04-26_R5_chain_extension: ledger sign + verify hooked into bridge_health (commit 7379ec17)
recent_changes_to_bridge_subsystem.2026-04-26_status_disambiguation: ec=0+no-sentinel → FAIL; ec!=0+fallback → OFFLINE-FALLBACK (HIT/ERROR split, commit 4a8eb529)
recent_changes_to_bridge_subsystem.2026-04-26_dockerfile_curl_wget: hexa-lang fe20e3dc adds curl+wget+ca-certificates to hexa-runner image (THIS SESSION)

physics_or_limits.container_curl_requirement: All 16 bridges pattern: exec("curl -s --max-time N '<url>' | head -c|n M") — no hexa-native HTTP. Removing curl from container = removing 16 bridges' live path with no diagnostic; fix requires apt-get install curl in Dockerfile.
physics_or_limits.OOM_4GB_cgroup_cap: Mac docker hard-landing pins mem=4g; OEIS b-files (1.2MB) and GWOSC jsonfull (2.7MB) trigger oom_kill during interp text.substring(j,j+1) byte-walk. Mitigated by shell-side `head -n 100` and `grep -E '<5 fields>'` BEFORE bytes reach interp (OPT-A pattern, oom_resolution ω-cycle).
physics_or_limits.selftest_timeout: 30s per bridge (gtimeout); max observed 5s (nanograv); total wall ~22s for 16 bridges sequential.
physics_or_limits.tampering_attack_surface: Bridges exec curl with raw URL — higher attack risk than falsifiers because external selftest paths could be subverted by .hexa rewrite. R1 sha256_16 pin defends silent-mutation; R5 ledger chain defends pin-table mutation.
physics_or_limits.no_secrets_today: All 16 bridges use unauthenticated public endpoints (OEIS, GWOSC, arXiv, SIMBAD, Wikipedia REST, OpenAlex, JPL Horizons, Planck, IceCube, NIST, Gaia, CERN OpenData, PubChem, UniProt). No API keys in any bridge — by-design free-tier access only.

friction_observed.F1_no_offline_mode_six_bridges: oeis/gw/arxiv/simbad/wikipedia/openalex have zero --no-fetch fallback; rate-limit or DNS outage = guaranteed FAIL. Asymmetric vs has-fallback 9 (codata/horizons/cmb/nanograv/icecube/nist_atomic/gaia/lhc/pubchem).
friction_observed.F2_uniprot_sentinel_bug: fallback fields fully populate yet sentinel emits ok=false because fetch was attempted-and-empty. Curl-orthogonal — survives Dockerfile patch.
friction_observed.F3_silent_fallback_masking: 9 has-fallback bridges currently PASS without ever validating the live path inside container; with curl present, latent live-side regressions (endpoint moves, schema drifts) will surface.
friction_observed.F4_no_rate_limit_handling: No bridge implements 429-detection or exponential backoff; first-request-fails = sentinel FAIL. Wikipedia REST and OpenAlex are most likely candidates to throttle on burst.
friction_observed.F5_no_response_cache: Every health-check run re-fetches identical URLs (e.g. OEIS A000396 b-file, GWOSC allevents catalog). Polite-API best-practice violated; cron @ 5min interval = 12 fetches/hr/bridge.
friction_observed.F6_no_retry_logic: Single attempt per fetch; transient TLS/DNS blip = FAIL. arxiv_realtime emits a hint to 'retry after 3s' but does not auto-retry.
friction_observed.F7_curl_only_wget_unused: Dockerfile fix added BOTH curl+wget but every bridge hardcodes 'curl' — wget is dead weight unless bridges adopt fallback ladder (try curl, then wget).
friction_observed.F8_baseline_drift_churn: openalex + lhc tampered episodes 2026-04-25 23:00-23:38 caused by legitimate edits ahead of baseline-refresh; today the workflow is 'commit edit → bridge_health goes red → rotate sha → green'. R5 chain is meant to formalise this but currently each rotate is manual.
friction_observed.F9_atlas_n_count_drift: GW selftest hardcodes total>=90 threshold; GWTC-3 currently ~370 events so safe, but a future catalog purge or endpoint switch would silently fail.

candidate_omega_axes.title: post-curl re-baseline
candidate_omega_axes.scope: rerun bridge_health.sh inside the new hexa-runner image; verify 7→1 FAIL transition (only uniprot remaining); confirm no latent live-fetch regressions surface in the previously-fallback-masked 9; refresh state/bridge_sha256.tsv pins if any bridges had curl-related code paths edited
candidate_omega_axes.deliverable: atlas_health_timeline.jsonl entry showing 15/16 PASS; sentinel comment 'post-curl re-baseline'; updated 2026-04-26_bridge_health_check.md table

candidate_omega_axes.title: uniprot sentinel-bug fix
candidate_omega_axes.scope: one-line edit in tool/uniprot_bridge.hexa: when fallback fields populate, set ok=true source=fallback (mirror gaia pattern). Independent of curl. Brings 15→16/16 PASS.
candidate_omega_axes.deliverable: diff + sha256.tsv refresh + green health check

candidate_omega_axes.title: rate-limit/backoff harmonization
candidate_omega_axes.scope: introduce shared retry-with-jitter helper (try N=3, base 200ms exp 2x with jitter); detect HTTP 429 + Retry-After header from curl -i; uplift the 6 requires-live bridges first, then the 9 has-fallback. Cap retries to 30s aggregate to honour selftest timeout.
candidate_omega_axes.deliverable: tool/_bridge_curl_retry.sh helper + per-bridge wiring + selftest unchanged sentinel

candidate_omega_axes.title: --no-fetch offline fallback for the requires-live 6
candidate_omega_axes.scope: add hardcoded n=6 anchor fixtures to oeis/gw/arxiv/simbad/wikipedia/openalex (perfect-number, GW150914+GW170817, top gr-qc paper, Sirius+M31, Pulsar+Perfect_number article extract, OpenAlex perfect-number entry). Selftest under --no-fetch returns these without network. Restores selftest meaningfulness during outages and brings rate-limit risk to zero on cron.
candidate_omega_axes.deliverable: 6 bridge edits + design doc 2026-04-XX_offline_fallback_propagation.md + sha256.tsv refresh

candidate_omega_axes.title: response cache + ETag honouring
candidate_omega_axes.scope: shared on-disk cache state/bridge_cache/<sha-of-url>.body with mtime-based TTL (default 6h); curl with --time-cond and -H 'If-None-Match' from cached ETag. Wikipedia/OpenAlex rate-limit pressure drops ~12x. Cache invalidation via state/bridge_cache_purge.sh cron.
candidate_omega_axes.deliverable: state/bridge_cache/ + tool/_bridge_cache.sh + per-bridge integration + LRU-purge cron

candidate_omega_axes.title: wget fallback ladder + auth-stub plumbing
candidate_omega_axes.scope: leverage the now-installed wget as second-tier fetcher when curl returns ec!=0 (exhaust curl retries → wget --tries=2 --timeout=10). Establish convention for future authenticated bridges (env-var BRIDGE_<NAME>_TOKEN → curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer ...'). No bridge today uses auth but the surface should be ready before adding e.g. SDSS/2MASS bridges.
candidate_omega_axes.deliverable: tool/_bridge_curl_retry.sh w/ wget tier + secret-management ADR

candidate_omega_axes.title: schema-drift sentinel + cross-bridge correlation v3
candidate_omega_axes.scope: extend selftest sentinels to emit response-shape hash (e.g. sha256 of sorted top-level JSON keys); bridge_health diff vs pinned shape → WARN status (raw 71 report-only). Pairs with cross_bridge_correlation_hunt v2 (open ω-cycle) to detect when an upstream renames a field — today such drift would silently degrade fallback quality without firing TAMPERED.

Dockerfile change (already landed in hexa-lang fe20e3dc this session)
running bridge_health.sh (Mac-side would mask the docker-container effect under audit)
